steps.luau_template:
-- AUTO-GENERATED by runner.py for step 'ocean' (primitive world.ocean)
local PARAMS = {
    center = Vector3.new(0, -15, 0),
    size_x = 600,
    size_z = 600,
    depth = 30
}

--!strict
-- PRIMITIVE: world.ocean
-- Lays terrain Water in a rectangular slab. Run before any island/landmass so subsequent
-- terrain fills overwrite the water where land emerges.
-- Expects global PARAMS:
--   center: Vector3?   default (0, -15, 0)
--   size_x: number?    default 600
--   size_z: number?    default 600
--   depth: number?     default 30  (slab thickness along Y)
-- Returns: { y_top: number — water surface y (top of the slab) }

local Terrain = game:GetService("Workspace").Terrain
local center = PARAMS.center or Vector3.new(0, -15, 0)
local sx = PARAMS.size_x or 600
local sz = PARAMS.size_z or 600
local d  = PARAMS.depth or 30

Terrain:FillBlock(CFrame.new(center.X, center.Y, center.Z), Vector3.new(sx, d, sz), Enum.Material.Water)

return {
    y_top = center.Y + d / 2,
}

-- AUTO-GENERATED by runner.py for step 'island' (primitive world.island)
local PARAMS = {
    name = "Island",
    center = Vector3.new(0, 0, 0),
    radius = 25,
    submerge_depth = 15,
    material = Enum.Material.Sand
}

--!strict
-- PRIMITIVE: world.island
-- Builds a sand-dome island via Terrain:FillBall. Water plane is a separate primitive (world.ocean).
-- Expects global PARAMS:
--   center: Vector3                  (world center of the ball)
--   radius: number                   (sphere radius in studs)
--   peak_height: number              (target peak above y=0, used to validate; informational)
--   material: Enum.Material?         (default Sand)
--   submerge_depth: number?          (how far below y=0 the ball center sits; default 15)
-- Returns (via `return` table consumed by execute_luau):
--   waterline_radius: number, peak_y: number, model_path: string

local Workspace = game:GetService("Workspace")
local Terrain = Workspace.Terrain

local center: Vector3 = PARAMS.center
local radius: number = PARAMS.radius
local material: Enum.Material = PARAMS.material or Enum.Material.Sand
local submergeDepth: number = PARAMS.submerge_depth or 15

-- Place ball so that radius-submergeDepth equals peak height above water
local ballCenter = Vector3.new(center.X, center.Y - submergeDepth, center.Z)
Terrain:FillBall(ballCenter, radius, material)

local peakY = ballCenter.Y + radius
local waterlineRadius = (radius * radius - submergeDepth * submergeDepth)
waterlineRadius = waterlineRadius > 0 and math.sqrt(waterlineRadius) or 0

-- Drop an invisible marker model so other primitives can anchor to this island by path
local marker = Instance.new("Model")
marker.Name = PARAMS.name or "Island"
local center_part = Instance.new("Part")
center_part.Name = "Anchor"
center_part.Anchored = true
center_part.CanCollide = false
center_part.Transparency = 1
center_part.Size = Vector3.new(1, 1, 1)
center_part.CFrame = CFrame.new(center.X, peakY, center.Z)
center_part.Parent = marker
marker.PrimaryPart = center_part
marker.Parent = Workspace

return {
    waterline_radius = waterlineRadius,
    peak_y = peakY,
    model_path = marker:GetFullName(),
}

exports.waterline_radius: number — horizontal radius at y=0, used by dock/bridge anchoring
exports.peak_y: number — top y of the island
exports.model_path: string — Workspace path of the island marker model (if created)

steps.luau_template:
-- AUTO-GENERATED by runner.py for step 'dock' (primitive world.dock)
local PARAMS = {
    name = "Dock",
    anchor_pos = Vector3.new(0, 0, 18),
    direction = Vector3.new(0, 0, 1),
    length = 20,
    width = 4,
    deck_top_y = 2,
    deck_thick = 1.5,
    post_spacing = 4,
    post_submerge_depth = 6
}

--!strict
-- PRIMITIVE: world.dock
-- Builds a wooden dock as a Model: deck plank + evenly spaced posts that extend underwater.
-- Direction-agnostic — works for docks pointing any horizontal direction.
-- Expects global PARAMS:
--   anchor_pos: Vector3        shore-end position (where dock meets land)
--   direction: Vector3         unit vector pointing into water (Y component ignored)
--   length: number?            default 20
--   width: number?             default 4
--   deck_top_y: number?        default 2 (above water y=0)
--   deck_thick: number?        default 1.5
--   post_spacing: number?      default 4
--   post_submerge_depth: number? default 6 (post bottom y = deck_top_y - this)
--   color_deck: Color3?
--   color_post: Color3?
--   name: string?              default "Dock"
-- Returns: { model_path, tip_position }

local Workspace = game:GetService("Workspace")

local anchorPos: Vector3 = PARAMS.anchor_pos
local rawDir: Vector3 = PARAMS.direction
local dir = Vector3.new(rawDir.X, 0, rawDir.Z)
if dir.Magnitude < 1e-4 then
    error("world.dock: direction must have non-zero horizontal component")
end
dir = dir.Unit

local length: number = PARAMS.length or 20
local width: number = PARAMS.width or 4
local deckTopY: number = PARAMS.deck_top_y or 2
local deckThick: number = PARAMS.deck_thick or 1.5
local postSpacing: number = PARAMS.post_spacing or 4
local postSubmerge: number = PARAMS.post_submerge_depth or 6
local colorDeck: Color3 = PARAMS.color_deck or Color3.fromRGB(150, 110, 70)
local colorPost: Color3 = PARAMS.color_post or Color3.fromRGB(95, 65, 40)

local model = Instance.new("Model")
model.Name = PARAMS.name or "Dock"

local centerPos = anchorPos + dir * (length / 2)
local deckCenterY = deckTopY - deckThick / 2
local deckCenter = Vector3.new(centerPos.X, deckCenterY, centerPos.Z)

-- Orient deck so its long axis (local +Z) aligns with dir
local deck = Instance.new("Part")
deck.Name = "Deck"
deck.Anchored = true
deck.Material = Enum.Material.WoodPlanks
deck.Color = colorDeck
deck.TopSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
deck.BottomSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
deck.Size = Vector3.new(width, deckThick, length)
deck.CFrame = CFrame.lookAt(deckCenter, deckCenter + dir)
deck.Parent = model

local postHeight = postSubmerge + deckThick
local postCenterY = deckTopY - postHeight / 2
local postSize = 0.6

local rightVec = Vector3.new(dir.Z, 0, -dir.X)
local numPosts = math.floor(length / postSpacing) + 1
for i = 0, numPosts - 1 do
    local along = anchorPos + dir * (i * postSpacing)
    for sign = -1, 1, 2 do
        local center = along + rightVec * (sign * width / 2)
        local post = Instance.new("Part")
        post.Name = string.format("Post_%d_%s", i, sign < 0 and "L" or "R")
        post.Anchored = true
        post.Material = Enum.Material.Wood
        post.Color = colorPost
        post.TopSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
        post.BottomSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
        post.Size = Vector3.new(postSize, postHeight, postSize)
        post.CFrame = CFrame.new(center.X, postCenterY, center.Z)
        post.Parent = model
    end
end

model.PrimaryPart = deck
model.Parent = Workspace

local tipPos = anchorPos + dir * length
tipPos = Vector3.new(tipPos.X, deckTopY, tipPos.Z)

return {
    model_path = model:GetFullName(),
    tip_position = tipPos,
}

exports.tip_position: Vector3 — world pos at the far end of the dock (good NPC/spawn anchor)

steps.luau_template:
-- AUTO-GENERATED by runner.py for step 'island_spawn' (primitive world.spawn_location)
local PARAMS = {
    name = "IslandSpawn",
    position = Vector3.new(0, 14, -8),
    material = Enum.Material.Pebble,
    color = Color3.fromRGB(130, 160, 130)
}

--!strict
-- PRIMITIVE: world.spawn_location
-- Places a single SpawnLocation as a player respawn anchor.
-- Expects global PARAMS:
--   position: Vector3      world position of the spawn center
--   name: string?          default "SpawnLocation"
--   size: Vector3?         default Vector3.new(6, 1, 6)
--   material: Material?    default Enum.Material.Pebble
--   color: Color3?         default Color3.fromRGB(130, 160, 130)
--   neutral: boolean?      default true
--   team_color: BrickColor? optional
-- Returns: { model_path: string }

local Workspace = game:GetService("Workspace")

local spawn = Instance.new("SpawnLocation")
spawn.Name = PARAMS.name or "SpawnLocation"
spawn.Anchored = true
spawn.Size = PARAMS.size or Vector3.new(6, 1, 6)
spawn.CFrame = CFrame.new(PARAMS.position)
spawn.TopSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
spawn.BottomSurface = Enum.SurfaceType.Smooth
spawn.Material = PARAMS.material or Enum.Material.Pebble
spawn.Color = PARAMS.color or Color3.fromRGB(130, 160, 130)
spawn.Neutral = PARAMS.neutral ~= false
spawn.Parent = Workspace

return {
    model_path = spawn:GetFullName(),
}

steps.source:
--!strict
-- TEMPLATE: script.weighted_random
-- Module returning a picker over a static weighted table. Use for fish tables, loot drops, rarity rolls, etc.
-- Substitution slots:
--   TABLE_LITERAL  -- a Luau array-of-tables literal; each entry MUST have a positive numeric .weight,
--                     plus any other fields the caller wants returned (name, color, etc.).

local Entries = {
    { name = "Anchovy", rarity = "common", weight = 60, sizeMin = 0.4, sizeMax = 0.9, color = Color3.fromRGB(180, 180, 170) },
    { name = "Perch", rarity = "common", weight = 25, sizeMin = 0.8, sizeMax = 1.4, color = Color3.fromRGB(120, 150, 90) },
    { name = "Bass", rarity = "uncommon", weight = 10, sizeMin = 1.2, sizeMax = 2.2, color = Color3.fromRGB(80, 110, 70) },
    { name = "Swordfish", rarity = "rare", weight = 4, sizeMin = 2.5, sizeMax = 4.0, color = Color3.fromRGB(60, 90, 130) },
    { name = "Golden Tuna", rarity = "legendary", weight = 1, sizeMin = 3.0, sizeMax = 5.0, color = Color3.fromRGB(220, 180, 60) }
}

local total = 0
for _, e in ipairs(Entries) do
    assert(type(e.weight) == "number" and e.weight > 0, "weighted_random: every entry needs a positive .weight")
    total = total + e.weight
end

local M = {}

function M.entries()
    return Entries
end

function M.pick(rngOverride: Random?): any
    local roll
    if rngOverride then
        roll = rngOverride:NextNumber() * total
    else
        roll = math.random() * total
    end
    local acc = 0
    for _, e in ipairs(Entries) do
        acc = acc + e.weight
        if roll <= acc then
            return e
        end
    end
    return Entries[#Entries]
end

return M

--!strict
-- TEMPLATE: ui.toast
-- LocalScript-side ModuleScript. Singleton transient text overlay on the bottom of the screen.
-- Designed to be required from any client script (e.g. tool LocalScripts).
-- Usage:
--   local Toast = require(ReplicatedStorage.UI.Toast)
--   Toast.show("BITE! Click to REEL!", Color3.fromRGB(255,190,60), 5)

local Players = game:GetService("Players")
local player = Players.LocalPlayer

local M = {}

local gui: ScreenGui? = nil
local label: TextLabel? = nil

local function ensureGui()
    if gui and gui.Parent then return end
    local pg = player:WaitForChild("PlayerGui")
    local existing = pg:FindFirstChild("ToastHUD")
    if existing then
        gui = existing :: ScreenGui
        label = (existing :: any):FindFirstChild("ToastLabel")
        return
    end

    local g = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
    g.Name = "ToastHUD"
    g.ResetOnSpawn = false
    g.IgnoreGuiInset = true
    g.Parent = pg

    local lbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
    lbl.Name = "ToastLabel"
    lbl.Size = UDim2.new(0.45, 0, 0, 60)
    lbl.Position = UDim2.new(0.275, 0, 0.72, 0)
    lbl.BackgroundTransparency = 0.35
    lbl.BackgroundColor3 = Color3.new(0, 0, 0)
    lbl.BorderSizePixel = 0
    lbl.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold
    lbl.TextScaled = true
    lbl.TextColor3 = Color3.new(1, 1, 1)
    lbl.Visible = false
    lbl.Parent = g

    gui = g
    label = lbl
end

function M.show(text: string, color: Color3?, durationSeconds: number?)
    ensureGui()
    if not label then return end
    label.Text = text
    label.TextColor3 = color or Color3.new(1, 1, 1)
    label.Visible = true
    if durationSeconds then
        local snapshot = text
        task.delay(durationSeconds, function()
            if label and label.Text == snapshot then label.Visible = false end
        end)
    end
end

function M.hide()
    if label then label.Visible = false end
end

return M
